title: dense brazilian beef stew
description: A hearty, slow-cooked stew originating from Brazil. Featuring a rich beef gravy, yuzu-cure, and a medley of spices.

ingredients.name: yuzu juice
ingredients.notes: use fresh yuzu for best flavor

ingredients.name: chicken broth
ingredients.notes: use low-sodium broth for best flavor

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Sear the beef chuck until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add more oil if necessary and saut3e the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the yuzu juice, brown sugar, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned beef to the pot and pour in the chicken and beef broths, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Add the tomato paste to the pot and stir to combine.
Stir in the cornstarch to thicken the stew, then serve hot.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
